headline: Inside Saint Petersburg's Immortal Regiment and its shift from tribute to propaganda
description: During the May 9 commemorations, Russian photographer Mary Gelman walked the streets of Vladimir Putin's hometown to take the pulse of a country sealed off from the world.

headline: Listed companies post record profits around the world
Iran says it sent response to US proposal via Pakistan
In Virginia, the world's data center capital faces mounting opposition from local residents
description: This East Coast US state is home to 12% of the world's capacity for the massive data centers essential to AI, which it has attracted largely through tax breaks. Local residents are especially concerned about their environmental impact.

headline: Energy transition becomes more accessible to the French middle class
description: Rising fossil fuel prices and falling used electric vehicle costs have made low-carbon solutions in housing and transport more attractive, according to a recent report by the Institute for Climate Economics.

headline: How much space should cities give to wild vegetation?
description: Bordeaux's new city council has launched a major program to weed sidewalks across the city, while in Montpellier, officials are considering expanding what it calls France's first 'wild district.'

headline: Women are featured on Cannes's poster, but still sidelined at the festival
description: The Cannes Film Festival's choice of a still from 'Thelma & Louise' for its official poster has drawn criticism from the feminist collective 50/50, which points to the persistently low number of female directors in the official selection.

Pedro Almodóvar still burns with desire for cinema
description: Born during Madrid's cultural awakening, his work is celebrated around the world. But, despite several appearances at the Cannes Film Festival, Pedro Almodóvar has never won the Palme d'Or. Now 76 years old, the pioneer of Spanish auteur cinema is back in competition with 'Bitter Christmas,' in which he delves into the labyrinth of creation.

headline: How chicken is taking over France's fast-food market
description: The number of fast-food chains dedicated to chicken is booming in France. At the heart of this success is affordable comfort food, offering an unbeatable quantity-for-price ratio at a time when purchasing power is on the decline.

headline: Military deterrence has fully entered 'third nuclear age'
description: India, Pakistan, Ukraine, North Korea, Iran: The military revolution has also impacted the nuclear weapon debate, notably with the end of transparency and new announcements of increases to global arsenals, including in France.
